summary: THE MPI-191 fix, carded ready-for-design-review: volume-pod hot-store — stage >=~15GB weight files to container disk, sticky per pod lifetime, LRU on real pressure only, volume stays durable library. Threshold not model-name (Q8 22GB paid same tax). Disk 50->80GB. Wrapper-owned, ships via publish-runtime. Full requirements + evidence + open questions in plan.md.

todo -> doing. Design settled w/ user: threshold=15GB PER FILE (TE=9GB out, Wan=14GB out, LTX-40GB only qualifies today); LAZY first-gen staging not boot-eager (boot cant know next model); STICKY + LRU-EVICT REQUIRED (2nd big model evicts if no room); disk STAYS 50GB (no payload change) but 15GB-threshold + disk-bump-rule to be DOCUMENTED durably. Whole fix = wrapper.py+start.sh+app preflight via publish-runtime.sh, NO image rebuild. Decisions appended to plan.md. 3 scout agents mapping resolveDeps consumers / wrapper machinery / dep file sizes.

CODE COMPLETE + offline-verified, awaiting publish+live. Wrapper (mpi-ci 05a44e5, v0.2.26): POST /wrapper/hot-store/ensure, LRU evict, disk-local state, sha-verify, SSE models:stage-*. App+route+doc (Cubric-Vision f43d631): _ensureRemoteHotStore remote-gen preflight (>=15GB filter, best-effort, awaited for toast) + /remote/hot-store/ensure proxy + runpod-remote-engine.md §5 hot-store+disk-budget rule. Offline: hotstore_selfcheck.py ALL PASS (sticky/LRU-evict/adopt/refuse/volume-untouched); sizeToGb filter (41GB in, 9.45+13.55 out); eslint clean; py_compile+node --check clean. Playbook >=15GB PING-USER gate shipped 1ad5b3b. maturity in-progress->validating. NOT YET PUBLISHED: publish-runtime.sh stable (production deploy, USER-run) then live: fresh volume pod, first LTX remote gen shows ~55s staging toast once + warm gap ~9s, 2nd gen no re-stage.

★ LIVE-PROVEN 2026-07-05 (5090 32GB, fresh volume, CPU-pod LTX install). Hot-store WORKS end-to-end: pod log [cubric] hot-store: staged ltx...bf16.safetensors to disk (42020150120 bytes) + POST /wrapper/hot-store/ensure 200. COLD first gen 2m10s (incl one-time 42GB stage, ~1m15s to stage-1 start). WARM: stage-1->2 gap ~9s (was ~36-39s), full warm 46s (was ~81s) = disk-parity = THE MPI-191 fix delivered ~4x. No re-stage on warm (sticky). bf16 42GB confirmed (GGUF gone, no regression). Toast-timing bug found+fixed: warm gen showed Preparing... toast though nothing copied -> added dryRun mode (wrapper 0.2.27 b299d85 + app 6fe8c2d), toast now cold-only. Self-check ALL PASS incl dryRun gate. NEEDS: republish wrapper 0.2.27 (USER) + one warm gen confirms no-toast. Then -> done.

CORRECTION — win is length-dependent, NOT a universal ~4x. Live 5090: SHORT video warm gap ~9s + full 46s (tier-1 page cache holds the 42GB transformer). But 5s VIDEO warm gap = 45s despite transformer ON DISK (confirmed staged, no toast). Math: 45s ~= reading 42GB from container disk at ~1GB/s = TIER 2. The 9s was TIER 1 (cache). Mechanism (matches perf-doc tier model page-cache->disk->volume): on a longer video the bigger latent working set EVICTS the 42GB transformer from page cache -> stage-2 re-fault falls to disk-read (~45s) not RAM (~9s). Hot-store IS working: it caps the worst case at tier 2 (~45s) vs tier 3 volume (~56s+ before). But tier-2 for a 42GB file is inherently ~45s. The ~9s dream needs TIER 1 = keep transformer resident/in-RAM (MPI-160 big-RAM floor, or aimdo keep-resident which the plan called a dead-end). 10s-video test pending to confirm disk-read floor (~45s flat) vs a second frame-scaling cost. Card STAYS validating; do NOT close as ~4x.

doing -> DONE. Hot-store SHIPPED + LIVE-PROVEN (5090): short warm gap ~9s (was ~36s), full warm 46s (was ~81s) = THE MPI-191 volume-read fix delivered. Wrapper 0.2.27 (dryRun toast-gate) PUBLISHED + verified on R2 pod.cubric.studio. Commits: mpi-ci b299d85 + Cubric-Vision f43d631/6fe8c2d. Self-check ALL PASS. The length-scaling long-video gap is a DISTINCT root cause (aimdo thrash) split to MPI-197 — NOT a MPI-194 regression. Accepted.
Archived completed card during board cleanup.
